checks.finding: All four elements present. (1) Metric row named: Target-Metric: implementation-report artifacts requiring a gate-phase path move/rename before the independent-evaluator launch boundary accepts them, per feature (count). (2) Baseline and target: Baseline: 9 (evidence-deep-verify, 2026-07-09: 8 task reports + 1 traceability addendum); Target: 0. (3) Cycle count: Horizon: next completed feature retrospective (one cycle). (4) Threshold: 'the proposal succeeds only if that count is 0', plus a secondary specific check (grep all 8 corrected plugin files for the flat-path pattern, confirm zero remaining occurrences outside the confirmed-excluded lite-gate/SKILL.md). No missing element.

Evidence scale: structural and systemic -- 8 installed plugin skill/reference/template locations (spanning 3 plugins: sdd-implementation, sdd-quality-loop, sdd-bootstrap) document the pre-canonicalization flat path, causing a bulk 9-file gate-phase rename this period alone (commit e3c148e). Change scope: 7 of the 8 flagged locations receive an identical one-line path correction each, and the 8th (sdd-lite/lite-gate) is confirmed out of scope with a specific stated reason (path-agnostic validator, no launch-boundary invocation) rather than silently omitted. Change scope matches evidence scope 1:1 (every flagged location addressed, none left unexplained). Proportional.

Scanned docs/workflow-improvements/ for Status: Verified WFIs: WFI-001, WFI-002, WFI-003, WFI-004 (all Verified). All four Proposed Change tables target only AGENTS.md subsections (high-risk-task preflight; review-gate precheck fallback; evidence report identity fields; post-review artifact freeze / provenance re-review). None touch any of the 7 plugins/sdd-implementation, sdd-quality-loop, or sdd-bootstrap files WFI-007 corrects. No overlap, no conflict. (WFI-005, Status: Applied not Verified and thus excluded by definition, touches deterministic-check-policy.md's placeholder-scan waiver text -- a different paragraph of the same file WFI-007 edits at line 140 for the report-path pattern; confirmed via git diff these are non-overlapping line ranges within the file, so even under a broader reading there is no textual conflict.)

Category is plugin-improvement (correctly classified: cross-plugin handoff gap between the implementation-phase plugin's own documented convention and the quality-loop plugin's independent-evaluator launch boundary). The WFI declares zero direct project-side Proposed Change ('WFI Direct Target (project-side): None') and routes the fix through the sanctioned GitHub-Issue lane, the designed mechanism for this category, not a misclassification. The root cause -- 8 plugin-internal files instructing implementers to write to a flat path the launch-boundary validator (plugins/sdd-quality-loop/scripts/validate-review-context-set.sh:265, confirmed unchanged in this diff) already rejects -- is baked into the plugin files' own instruction text; no AGENTS.md-level rule could correct 8 separate skill/reference/template files read verbatim by implementation-phase workers. Confirmed via `git diff origin/main -- plugins/`: all 7 corrected files match the 7 'Same path correction' rows one-for-one, and the 8th row's 'No change' claim for lite-gate/SKILL.md is confirmed (not in the diff), demonstrating the fix is concretely achievable and already applied through the declared mechanism.

Scanned Root Cause Hypothesis, Proposed Change Change Description column, and Expected Effect against every forbidden term in wfi-category-guide.md Section 2. All matches for plugin-specific strings (implement-task/SKILL.md, agent-delegation-policy.md, implementation-policy.md, implement-tasks/SKILL.md, visual-verify-loop/SKILL.md, deterministic-check-policy.md, AGENTS.template.md, lite-gate/SKILL.md) fall exclusively inside the Target File column, matching the WFI-005 precedent for literal path citations. Prose uses only generic terms ('independent-evaluator launch boundary', 'implementation-phase plugin', 'lightweight track' -- the correct required substitution for sdd-lite). No forbidden term newly introduced by the Cycle 1 restructuring; the new 'WFI Direct Target' / 'GitHub-Issue Lane' subheadings and surrounding prose use only 'plugins/' as a generic directory noun and cross-references to WFI-004 and the category guide.

Target is 0 path-move renames next feature, against a baseline of 9 this period. The friction is a deterministic documentation-vs-validator mismatch, not a probabilistic behavioral pattern: once all 8 flagged locations are corrected to match the launch boundary's existing (unchanged) requirement, there is no remaining mechanism that would produce a mismatch, since the validator itself is confirmed unchanged. Driving a purely documentation-caused defect to 0 after the documentation is fixed is plausible, not optimistic. No advisory needed.

Category: plugin-improvement, so this check applies. Problem Evidence summarizes cleanly in 2-3 generic sentences (documentation/validator path contradiction causing bulk gate-phase renames). The GitHub-Issue Lane Proposed Change table has 8 rows (7 corrections + 1 explicit out-of-scope confirmation) with concrete, generic, non-vague Change Description text sufficient to populate the Section 4 issue template verbatim. Expected Effect states a quantitative target (9 to 0) plus an explicit non-decreasing-guard statement. No row would produce an empty or vague issue body.

Meta-Change: true, strict lane applies. (1) Easier-to-satisfy: NO. Confirmed via `git diff origin/main -- plugins/sdd-quality-loop/scripts/validate-review-context-set.sh`: the launch-boundary script is byte-for-byte unchanged (empty diff). The 7 corrected files only align documentation prose with the boundary's existing, already-stricter requirement; no gate, grader, or threshold is loosened -- if anything, path-matching failures should become rarer, tightening observed compliance, not loosening it. (2) Non-decreasing guard: the full plugins/ diff touches no script, hook, or validator logic in any of the 7 files -- deterministic-check-policy.md's edit is a documentation-only path-pattern correction (confirmed: no script parses this file by name; check-task-state.sh uses its own directory-default logic, not this markdown text). 0 gates/checks added, 0 removed; count unchanged, consistent with the WFI's own claim. (3) Target-Metric independence: Target-Metric (path-move-rename count, observed via git rename-commit history and gate-report PATH-failure records at the next retrospective) is computed by external observation of git history and gate reports, not by any of the 7 files this WFI modifies or by the unmodified validator script. The instrument that computes the Target-Metric is untouched by this change.
